summary: Pure test-code update. The three required record_test.go edits match the task spec exactly: +2 formula for Test_app_record (meta + sysinfo per tick), wantN/wantV decrement/increment across every Test_filterViews row, and TestFilterViews_NotRecordable inverted to assert procpidstat passes through filterViews. The supplementary TestFilterViews_dropsExplicitNotRecordable preserves coverage of the NotRecordable=true drop branch (production code path at record.go:210) that would otherwise be untested after task 03. The new Test_app_doReport_procpidstat exercises the full readTar -> processData pipeline against an in-memory tar with two ticks (meta + procpidstat + sysinfo per tick), matching the per-tick layout produced by tarRecorder.write(). Test_describeReport gains the procpidstat case. Comments are accurate and reference the correct task numbers. No production code touched; the task constraint is honored.

suggestions.suggestion: The column header slice in Test_app_doReport_procpidstat is a copy-paste of internal/stat.procPidResultCols. If the canonical header is ever reordered, this fixture will drift silently — the test will still pass because Compare uses positional indices, not column names. Consider importing the canonical slice (or asserting equality against it) so a header drift fails this test loudly.
suggestions.benefit: Pins the fixture to the production header definition. Reduces the chance of a misleading green test after a header reorder.

suggestions.suggestion: The first tick's row values (statPrev) are passed through mkRow with the same shape as snapshot 2, but the comment says 'discarded as prev by processData's first-snapshot rule, values irrelevant beyond shape'. Since procpidstat's DiffIntvl is [0,0] (no diff — delta is curr verbatim), the prev values truly do not affect output; the test relies on this. A one-line note in the comment that DiffIntvl=[0,0] is the reason values are pass-through would make the contract explicit.
suggestions.benefit: Documents the implicit dependency on procpidstat's diff-free configuration. If DiffIntvl ever changes, the test author will know to revisit the fixture.

suggestions.suggestion: Comment 'sysinfo is emitted unconditionally so the report pipeline always has CLK_TCK/cpu_count' is accurate for the current tarRecorder.write() (record/recorder.go line 262). If the recorder were ever changed to skip sysinfo in remote mode, this test's +2 formula would over-count. Not actionable today; flagging for awareness.
suggestions.benefit: Future-proofing note. No code change required now.

checklist.Test_app_record_formula_plus2: PASS — countRecordable(view.New()) + 2 matches tarRecorder.write() output (stats + meta + sysinfo per tick)
checklist.Test_filterViews_table_updated: PASS — all six rows decremented wantN by 1, incremented wantV by 1; comment updated; arithmetic internally consistent with view.New() having 22 recordable views
checklist.TestFilterViews_NotRecordable_inverted: PASS — view constructed with NotRecordable=false; assertions n==0, len(v)==1, Contains(v, procpidstat), assert.False(pp.NotRecordable), Ncols==19 all correct
checklist.TestFilterViews_dropsExplicitNotRecordab: PASS — new test pins the NotRecordable=true drop branch (record.go:210) that would otherwise have zero coverage
checklist.Test_app_doReport_procpidstat_exists: PASS — exercises doReport end-to-end on in-memory tar; asserts non-empty output, '/' in timestamp line, 'SELECT 1' from data row
checklist.no_production_code_changes: PASS — git diff HEAD~1 shows only record_test.go and report_test.go modified
